title: Local review moderator should merge duplicate routed findings into one final record
description: A sanitized local-review scenario where the reviewer, skeptic, and architect all surface the same underlying concern through schema-conformant handoff records. Once moderator routing is enabled, the final adjudication owner should merge those duplicates into one final finding that preserves the strongest evidence and combined role attribution.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models duplicate routed concerns that should converge into one moderated record rather than surviving as repeated issues with slightly different wording.

mustCatch.description: The final adjudication owner should merge duplicate routed findings into one final record instead of emitting the same concern multiple times under different wording.
When duplicate records merge, the surviving final record should preserve the strongest evidence and combined role attribution rather than keeping the weakest version.

mustNotFlag.description: The final output must not contain two separate findings for the same underlying concern once moderator routing is enabled.

notes: This is a staged benchmark for the future moderator-routed workflow. Keep it ready so the advocate-to-moderator routing swap has an explicit duplicate-merge quality gate.
